---
title: "API reference"
---

The public API is everything exported from the top-level `cositos` package. It splits
into six concerns: **serialization**, **embedding**, the **`Widget` façade**, the
**`Transport` seam**, the **protocol core**, and **buffers**. Every symbol below is
importable directly, e.g. `from cositos import dump_document`.

The behaviour behind these functions is pinned by the living
[specifications](specs.qmd) (protocol, serialization, embed) — the same contracts the
test suite and any cross-language port certify against.

## Serialization

Persist a widget (or a composed graph of widgets) to plain JSON and rebuild it later.
Round-trips are lossless, including binary buffers (base64-encoded per the widget
state-format v2 schema). See the [serialization spec](specs.qmd#serialization).

| Function | Signature | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `dump_model` | `dump_model(entry) -> tuple[str, Record]` | Serialize one `(model_id, state)` entry to a record. |
| `load_model` | `load_model(item) -> tuple[str, dict]` | Inverse of `dump_model`. |
| `dump_document` | `dump_document(entries) -> Document` | Serialize many entries into a v2 Widget-State document envelope. |
| `load_document` | `load_document(doc) -> list[tuple[str, dict]]` | Inverse of `dump_document`. |

```python
from cositos import dump_document, load_document

doc = dump_document([("counter", {"_esm": ESM, "n": 3})])
# doc is a JSON-serializable dict; write it, ship it, reload it:
entries = load_document(doc)          # [("counter", {"_esm": ..., "n": 3})]
```

## Embedding

Render a serialized `Document` to self-contained HTML — no kernel required. The anywidget
model/view resolves from a CDN at display time, so no frontend is bundled. See the
[embed spec](specs.qmd#embed).

| Function | Signature | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `embed_snippet` | `embed_snippet(document, *, views=None, requirejs=True, html_manager_version="1") -> str` | Loader + state + view `<script>`s, no page wrapper — drop into an existing page (e.g. a Quarto cell). |
| `embed_html` | `embed_html(document, *, views=None, title="cositos widgets", requirejs=True, html_manager_version="1") -> str` | A complete standalone HTML page. |
| `write_html` | `write_html(path, document, **kwargs) -> None` | Write `embed_html` output to `path`. |

`views` selects which model ids get a rendered view (default: every model). The full
state is always embedded so a referenced model is present for a container to resolve.
A reference (`"IPY_MODEL_<id>"`) only resolves to a child model when the *holding* model
declares a widget-reference trait; plain anywidget (`AnyModel`) widgets do not, so refs
between them stay literal strings (use a `@jupyter-widgets/controls` container to compose
— see `examples/composition/`). Embedded JSON is
script-escaped so a `</script>` inside widget state can't break out of the page.

```python
from cositos import dump_document, write_html

doc = dump_document([("m", {"_esm": ESM, "n": 3})])
write_html("out.html", doc)           # open in any browser (needs internet for the CDN)
```

## The `Widget` façade

`Widget` ties a host state object to a `Transport`. It is deliberately minimal — **no
observer autodetection**: the host decides when state changed and calls `send_state`.

```python
Widget(
    transport,                 # a Transport adapter (see below)
    get_state,                 # () -> dict — current full state (must include "_esm")
    set_state=None,            # (dict) -> None — apply inbound state; omit to ignore updates
    model_id="",               # comm id, for the display mimebundle
    on_custom=None,            # (content, buffers) -> None — handle custom messages
)
```

| Method | Purpose |
|---|---|
| `open()` | Send `comm_open`, adopt the transport's real comm id, start listening (if the transport `supports_receive`). |
| `send_state(include=None)` | Send an `update` with the full state, or only the `include` keys. |
| `send_custom(content, buffers=None)` | Send a `custom` message (`model.on("msg:custom")` on the frontend). |
| `close()` | Send `comm_close`. |
| `mimebundle(repr_text="")` | The widget-view mimebundle for display. |
| `_repr_mimebundle_(...)` | Jupyter display hook — rendering the widget opens its comm, then returns the view bundle. |

## The `Transport` seam

`Transport` is the **only** kernel-facing interface the core depends on. Each host
(Python `comm`, Deno `Deno.jupyter.broadcast`, IJulia, dotnet-interactive) supplies a
thin adapter; the core contains no I/O. It is a
[`typing.Protocol`](https://docs.python.org/3/library/typing.html#typing.Protocol) —
implement it structurally, no subclassing required.

```python
@runtime_checkable
class Transport(Protocol):
    supports_receive: bool     # False on broadcast-only kernels (one-way widgets)

    def send(self, msg_type, content, buffers=None, metadata=None) -> None: ...
    def on_message(self, callback) -> None: ...
    # comm_id: str — the real comm id, read defensively and adopted on open()
    #   (not part of the Protocol; supplied by adapters whose comm generates an id)
```

## Protocol core

Pure message shaping and parsing — the language-neutral heart of the library. See the
[protocol spec](specs.qmd#protocol).

| Symbol | Signature | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `PROTOCOL_VERSION` | `str` (`"2.1.0"`) | The widget messaging protocol version cositos targets. |
| `build_comm_open` | `build_comm_open(state) -> tuple[dict, list, dict]` | Build the `comm_open` payload (data, buffers, metadata). |
| `build_update` | `build_update(state) -> tuple[dict, list]` | Build an `update` (`comm_msg`) payload. |
| `build_custom` | `build_custom(content) -> dict` | Build a `custom` message payload. |
| `parse_message` | `parse_message(data)` → `Update` \| `RequestState` \| `Custom` \| `Ignored` | Parse an inbound `comm_msg` into a typed event. |

`parse_message` returns one of four typed events: `Update{state, buffer_paths}`,
`RequestState`, `Custom{content}`, or `Ignored{method}`. An unknown or missing `method`
yields `Ignored` (it never raises), matching ipywidgets' forward-compatible dispatch — the
caller no-ops on it (see the [protocol spec](specs.qmd#protocol)).

## Buffers

Split binary values out of state and merge them back — a faithful port of the widget
protocol v2 nested-buffer rules.

| Function | Signature | Purpose |
|---|---|---|
| `remove_buffers` | `remove_buffers(state) -> tuple[state, buffer_paths, buffers]` | Extract binary values, replacing each with a hole in the state tree. |
| `put_buffers` | `put_buffers(state, buffer_paths, buffers) -> None` | Inverse; mutates `state` in place. |
